heading: Membership
label: 170103 Membership
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)17010336:1157., , .Aug. 11, 1971Pub. L. 92–93, § 785 Stat. 318In clause (1), the words “citizen of the United States” are substituted for “American citizen” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.Clause (3)(A) is substituted for “Service with the Armed Forces must have been terminated by discharge or separation from service under conditions other than dishonorable” to eliminate unnecessary words.Clause (3)(B) is substituted for “, That persons otherwise eligible for membership who are on active duty or who must continue to serve after the cessation of hostilities are also eligible for membership” to eliminate unnecessary words.Provided, however

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of authorities, functions, personnel, and assets of the Coast Guard, including the authorities and functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating thereto, to the Department of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 468(b), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6
amendments: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)17010336:1157., , .Aug. 11, 1971Pub. L. 92–93, § 785 Stat. 318In clause (1), the words “citizen of the United States” are substituted for “American citizen” for consistency in the revised title and with other titles of the United States Code.Clause (3)(A) is substituted for “Service with the Armed Forces must have been terminated by discharge or separation from service under conditions other than dishonorable” to eliminate unnecessary words.Clause (3)(B) is substituted for “, That persons otherwise eligible for membership who are on active duty or who must continue to serve after the cessation of hostilities are also eligible for membership” to eliminate unnecessary words.Provided, however

children.text: is a citizen of the United States;

was regularly enlisted, inducted, or commissioned, and was accepted for or on active duty, in the Army, Navy, Marine Corps, Air Force, or Coast Guard of the United States or an ally of the United States;

children.label: A
children.text: was separated from service in the Armed Forces under conditions other than dishonorable; or

children.label: B
children.text: is on active duty or must continue to serve after the cessation of hostilities; and
has suffered a spinal cord injury or disease, whether or not service connected in origin.
